DECLARATION OF ERIKA K. BORNSTEIN IN SUPPORT OF SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY GALUSKY TO PROVIDE FUR THERE RESPONSES AT HIS CONTINUED DEPOSITION AND FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 100039238) FILED BY DEFENDANT VANESS, NINA ASHLEY
EXHIBITS A-G (TRANSACTION ID # 100039238) FILED BY DEFENDANT VANESS, NINA ASHLEY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NINA VANESS' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY GALUSKY TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES AT HIS CONTINUED DEPOSITION AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING IN IT'S ENTIRETY. SANCTIONS DENIED. DEFENDANT NINA VANESS' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY GALUSKY TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES AT HIS CONTINUED DEPOSITION AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED AND MR. GALUSKY'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS ALSO DENIED. NONE OF THE SIX SETS OF DEPOSITION QUESTIONS IDENTIFIED IN MS. VANESS'STATEMENT OF ITEMS IN DISPUTE SEEK SUFFICIENTLY PROBATIVE INFORMATION TO OVERRIDE MR. GALUSKY'S MEDICAL PRIVACY PRIVILEGE. WHILE IT IS POSSIBLE THAT INFORMATION ABOUT OTHER PORTIONS OF MR. GALUSKY'S BODY THAN THOSE PORTIONS ALLEGEDLY INJURED IN THE ACCIDENT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS CASE MAY BE DISCOVERABLE ON THE ISSUE OF GENERAL DAMAGES, MS. VANESS HAS OFFERED NO FACTS TO WARRANT REQUIRING MR. GALUSKY TO ANSWER THE QUESTIONS HE DECLINED TO ANSWER AT HIS DEPOSITION. ALTHOUGH IT IS A CLOSE CALL, THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE WAS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR THE MOTION. MS. VANESS' COUNSEL IS ADMONISHED THAT SHE WAS NOT GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE ANY FURTHER PAPERS AFTER THE HEARING BEFORE THE JUDGE PRO TEM AND SHOULD NOT HAVE FILED ANY FURTHER PAPERS WITHOUT RECEIVING LEAVE TO DO SO. COUNSEL FOR MR. GALLUSKY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-24-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Timothy Galusky To Provide Further Responses At His Continued Deposition And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Statement Of Items; Declaration Of Erika K. Bornstein In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jan-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michelle Weiss, Esq. 415-466-8717 of Bay Area Bicycle Law for Plaintiff.
Erika Bornstein, Esq. 925-825-9500 of the Law Office of Catherine Walsh for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues there was no substantial meet and confer. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT NINA VANESS' Motion To Compel Plaintiff Timothy Galusky To Provide Further Responses At His Continued Deposition And For Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling in it's entirety. Sanctions Denied. Defendant Nina Vaness' motion to compel plaintiff Timothy Galusky to provide further responses at his continued deposition and for monetary sanctions is denied and Mr. Galusky's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. None of the six sets of deposition questions identified in Ms. Vaness'statement of items in dispute seek sufficiently probative information to override Mr. Galusky's medical privacy privilege. While it is possible that information about other portions of Mr. Galusky's body than those portions allegedly injured in the accident that is the subject of this case may be discoverable on the issue of general damages, Ms. Vaness has offered no facts to warrant requiring Mr. Galusky to answer the questions he declined to answer at his deposition. Although it is a close call, the court finds that there was substantial justification for the motion. Ms. Vaness' counsel is admonished that she was not given leave to file any further papers after the hearing before the Judge Pro Tem and should not have filed any further papers without receiving leave to do so.

Counsel for Mr. Gallusky is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
